description: In this tour, you'll get a unique once in a lifetime experience, with a non-touristy and not crowded trail that is Off The Beaten Path. Featuring Secluded Limestone Environment. Full All-around Adventure that includes all aspects of hiking.

title: San Juan Self-Guided Walking Audio Tour
description:
Embark on a walking tour of San Juan, full of majestic architecture, breathtaking views, and embattled history. Behold the largest Spanish-built fortification in the Americas. Hear stories of pirate battles, greed, and power featuring figures like Sir Francis Drake. Stroll down ancient city streets rich with culture and history, then walk along this beautiful city's old walls and enjoy some gentle ocean views.

After booking, check your email/text to download the separate Audio Tour Guide App by Action while connected to WiFi or mobile data. Enter the password, download the tour, and enjoy it offline. Follow the audio instructions and route from the designated starting point.

New, Lifetime access, no expiry. Use it anytime, on any trip, as many times as you want.

This isn't an entrance ticket. Check opening hours before your visit.

title: Private 30+Minute Photoshoot in Old San Juan (Photo shoot)
description: Old San Juan can be quite warm! If a full tour isn't what you are looking for, consider this alternative: a mini photo session in this UNESCO historic district. Capture professional shots of yourself and loved ones quickly and affordably, then continue your exploration of Old San Juan at your own pace.

title: Half Day San Juan Driven Tour
description:
Enjoy a customized 4 hours driven tour with stops at Old San Juan—its historic architecture, churches,souvenirs shops,Forts, houses, and plazas dating back to the 1500s.

You’ll experience the contrast between Old San Juan and the “New San Juan” when we stop at colourful Cerra Street to admire its vibrant street art. We’ll also visit the lively Santurce Market, La Placita, and drive through Villa Palmera and Isla Verde, where you can enjoy the beauty of Isla Verde Beach.

Our route also takes us through the elegant Condado area, showcasing its modern architecture, luxury hotels, and stylish condos.

Best of all, we’ll explore at your pace and according to your preferences, making this tour uniquely yours.

Remember, I can tailor the tour to suit your pace and preferences

title: Canopy Rush 5-Ziplines Adventure with Transportation Included
description:
The tour is located just a short drive from the San Juan metro area, making it one of the most accessible adventure experiences for visitors who want excitement without long travel times. Stress-free roundtrip transportation included.

The experience takes place on a large, historic Puerto Rican plantation, adding heritage, character, and a strong sense of place that distinguishes it from standard zipline parks.

Guests may observe local wildlife as they travel through the plantation grounds, turning the approach to the canopy into part of the adventure.

The ziplines run through a lush green forest canopy, offering expansive natural views and an authentic connection to Puerto Rico’s landscape.

Short, scenic hikes between platforms allow guests to appreciate the terrain, explore the environment, and experience the forest from multiple perspectives.

A unique photo opportunity awaits at the PR flag chair.

title: San Juan Nightlife Guided Experience; Art & Music Tour
description:
We provide a seamless and elevated experience with a professional driver and certified tour guide, ensuring comfort, safety, and local expertise throughout your journey.

Enjoy round-trip transportation in a modern, air-conditioned minivan, designed for a smooth and relaxing ride.

Your tour takes you through the best nightlife spots in San Juan, where you’ll safely explore the city’s vibrant atmosphere after dark.

Immerse yourself in the rich culture of Puerto Rico through art, live music, and authentic local connections, creating a memorable and engaging experience beyond the typical tour.

title: Salsa Class & Nightlife Experience in San Juan
description:
This class comes from a place of love and reconnection with culture. Salsa Boriken brings salsa back to the people: reviving the culture, honoring tradition, and keeping it fresh for a new generation.
By mixing the flavor of street salsa with the smooth flow of studio style, and small classes for personal attention, we teach moves that feel fun, not forced. Find your rhythm that feels good, looks good, and keep the soul of salsa alive!

title: Zipline Sky Bull in Urban Environment in Toroverde urban park San Juan
description: You will enjoy three courses of parallel cables of ziplines between 150 and 400 feet in length. Starting from the launch tower at a height of 90 feet, moving between ziplines on a sky ride throughout the T-Mobile District enjoying a show of lights and digital screens. You will be on a system called the Safe Roller which will keep you connected to the main lines at all times until the end of the ride.

title: Paddleboard Adventure: Explore Marine Life in Condado Lagoon
description:
Unlike any other city escape, our paddleboard experience puts you heart-to-heart with nature. We provide a professional basics class to ensure you feel confident on the water, followed by an exploration of the Condado Lagoon. What truly sets us apart is the unique opportunity to paddle alongside manatees, sea turtles, and rays while enjoying the most iconic views of the San Juan skyline. It's the perfect balance of adventure, tranquility, and wildlife spotting.

No Experience Needed: Perfect for first-timers! We provide a complimentary 5-to-10 minute safety and skills briefing before you hit the water.

Pro Instruction: Our friendly staff will teach you the best techniques to balance, paddle, and turn so you can feel confident and secure.

Other things to note:
We recommend that you don't take anything into the water that you don't want to lose (jewelry etc...) We have an indoor facility to leave your things. 

**Maximum weight of: 220lb**

title: Discover Fajardo: 2-Tank Scuba Diving
description:
Nothing compares to the "weightless" exhilaration of breathing underwater. Only a diver knows the feeling! The PADI Discover Scuba Diving program lets you experience the thrill of diving under the supervision of a PADI Instructor. During your adventure, you'll master some basic concepts and scuba skills before heading off on an open water dive. Your open water dive takes you as deep as 12 metres/40 feet! (Of course this depends on where you dive.)

You must check-in by 7:30. If you are late and miss the boat, a refund will not be granted.

title: San Juan PR Transfer in Luxury SUV (Airports,Ports,Hotel,Airbnb)
description: We are a luxury transportation company based in San Juan, Puerto Rico. Elevate your travel experience with our top-notch services, designed to ensure your journey with us is exceptional. Whether you are celebrating a special occasion, needing seamless airport transportation, attending a business meeting, or planning a night out on the town, our professional chauffeurs and immaculate vehicles are ready to serve you.​

images.caption: Hotel

title: El Yunque Off the Beaten Path Tour with Transportation
description:
Feeling adventurous? Dare to explore a different zone of El Yunque, while enjoying in a safe and guided way, the different water activities this natural park has to offer.

Experience the beauty of El Yunque Rainforest while hiking, river trekking and rock climbing.  Enjoy spotting its flora and fauna, scenic views and gorgeous hidden waterfalls, natural pools and slides.

This is a moderate to strenuous activity and may not be suitable for everyone.  Please read entire description before booking in order to avoid any inconveniences. 
For an educational, low impact tour please see our other version "El Yunque National Forest Half-Day Tour".

title: FlyDining: Brunch with Ocean Views & Tropical Breeze (3 Courses)
description: The activity distinguishes itself by combining the thrill of elevated dining with the luxury of gourmet cuisine. Guests are treated to a meal suspended high above the ground, offering an adrenaline rush along with stunning panoramic views that a traditional dining setting simply cannot match. The experience is not just about the height; it's about savoring high-quality, expertly prepared dishes while floating in the sky. Safety and comfort are paramount, ensuring a peaceful yet exhilarating dining experience. Ideal for special occasions or making any day memorable, this unique adventure adds an exclusive touch to any celebration. It's more than a meal; it's an unforgettable journey that elevates dining to new heights.

title: VIP Private Floating Lounge & Prosecco in Condado
description: The ultimate private oasis in San Juan. Our exclusive floating furniture is the only one of its kind in the Condado Lagoon, offering a private 'island' for you and your group of up to 6 people. Whether you are celebrating a special occasion or just want a luxury escape, our lounge provides the perfect blend of relaxation and party vibes. With premium Prosecco and a curated charcuterie board included, you don't just rent a float—you own the lagoon for two hours

images.caption: The ultimate floating party for groups up to 6 people.

title: Combo El Yunque Rainforest and San Juan Snorkeling with Transport
description: Immerse yourself in an unforgettable adventure with our El Yunque rainforest and Puerto Rico Caribbean San Juan snorkeling combo tour! Delve into the lush wonders of El Yunque rainforest, where nature trails, waterfalls, and panoramic vistas await. Then, dive into the crystal-clear waters for a snorkeling from shore experience like no other. Discover the beauty of both land and sea in one remarkable journey, led by expert guides who ensure an unforgettable experience. Book now and make memories that will last a lifetime!

title: Rainforest Nature Walk & Luquillo Beach Combo
description:
Our tour guide will meet you at the pickup location, at the previously accorded pickup time (call us for confirmation).We'll then go east for around 45 minutes to El Yunque. Throughout the ride, live commentary and interpretation of your surroundings, as well as information on our Puerto Rican heritage, can be provided in both English and Spanish.
We will see La Coca Falls and Yokahu Tower once we get in the Rainforest. Then, for around 20 minutes, you'll walk through the flora till you reach the river stream on a nature walk path (look out for slippery, uneven ground). Remember to bring your towel and swimsuit! (Swimming is dependent on the weather.)
After the rainforest, we'll board the bus again and travel to Luquillo Beach and Kiosks, where we'll stay for two hours so you can eat or relax on the beach before returning.
Finally, we'll wrap up the day by returning guests to their original pick-up location.

title: El Yunque Tour with Luquillo Lunch Stop and Transport: 6 ppl Max
description:
This activity is unique because it offers visitors the chance to explore a hidden gem in Puerto Rico that few tourists ever get to experience. The La Tinajas adventure takes you off the beaten path and into the heart of the rainforest, where you'll hike through challenging terrain, climb rocks, and rappel to reach the crystal-clear waters of the Rio La Tinajas.

Once you reach the river, you'll have the chance to cool off in the refreshing waters, dive into natural pools, explore the twists and turns of the river, and even try your hand at cliff jumping or swinging. The adventure offers an extra dose of adrenaline for those seeking a thrill, while our guides will be with you every step of the way to ensure your safety and share their knowledge of the ecosystem.

After the adventure, you'll get to enjoy a delicious and authentic Puerto Rican meal at a local restaurant, providing a cultural experience and a taste of grandma-style cooking.

title: Golden Hour Beach Photo Shoot in San Juan
description:
With 15+ years of experience and a few notable publications under my belt, I’m excited to bring my passion for photography to the stunning shores of Isla del Encanto—and to photograph you!

We’ll meet in the lobby of La Concha Renaissance, then take a short walk to one of Puerto Rico’s hidden gems: a pristine beach with soft, golden sand and sapphire-blue waves. It’s a breathtaking setting perfect for singles, couples, families, or anyone wanting to capture a special moment.

Whether you’re celebrating love, friendship, or simply your time in paradise, this photo shoot is a beautiful and lasting way to remember your trip.

Thanks for choosing me to capture your memories—I look forward to photographing you in paradise!

title: El Yunque National Rainforest and Luquillo Beach Tour
description:
Experience the natural beauty of El Yunque National Forest on a guided adventure from San Juan. After pickup from our designated locations, journey into the rainforest and drive nearly 1,600 feet above sea level for breathtaking views of Puerto Rico’s east coast from the Yokahú Observation Tower.
Stop at the iconic La Coca Falls for photos before heading on a short 15-minute hike to the beautiful Mameyes river, where you can swim in refreshing natural pools (weather permitting). 

After approximately two hours exploring the rainforest, we’ll continue to Luquillo Beach and the famous kiosk area, where you can relax on the beach, enjoy local food and drinks, or simply soak in the coastal atmosphere.

images.caption: Views

title: Famous Guavate Pork Road & Charco Azul Food & River Tour
description: This tour is simply a must for all food lovers. This day trip combines two of Puerto Rico's most popular activities - eating and pristine rivers - into one unforgettable adventure. During this tour we will visit Guavate (Puerto Rico's famous Pig Road) and Charco Azul, which makes this tour suitable and fun for all ages. It has something for adventure lovers and relaxation enthusiasts alike, without leaving behind the foodies. This is a great tour for people of all ages as long as they can walk with no problems. Charco Azul is located in Carite Rainforest. The waters are pristine blue and absolutely breathtaking. The walk through the rainforest is quite memorable for the enchanting Coqui noises and the absolutely breathtaking variety and beauty of the plants in the area. Once we get to Charco Azul we will swim in cool tropical waters that are so clean and clear that no goggles are needed for visibility underwater. Afterwards we will eat some of the most delicious food ever.

title: The Ultimate Self-Guided Hidden Gems Tour in Puerto Rico
description:
Discover the 20 most spectacular spots locals love — and tourists almost never find.

Hidden and famous beaches, crystal-clear rivers, magical caves, and must visit spots in Old San Juan. All packed into one downloadable digital map.

What do you get?

A ready-to-use PDF with:

• Stunning photos of each location
• Local tips to help you prepare
• Direct links to Google Maps (just one click and you're on your way!)

This isn’t a guided tour. It’s total freedom.
You choose when and where to go. All you need is a rental car… and this map.

Perfect for couples, families, and adventurers who don’t want to waste time scrolling through blogs or asking around what to do.

title: Old San Juan Private Tour with Barrilito Rum Experience
description: Join us and visit Hacienda Santa Ana, the home of the world’s best sipping rum! Submerge into the history of the heritage of the Fernandez Family, their residence and the Rum Factory. Come and see the bottling, maceration and aging process of the oldest rum brand in Puerto Rico with barrels dating all the way back to 1952. After that enjoy a private historical tour of the Old City of San Juan to end a day full of rum and history of the beautiful island of Puerto Rico.

title: Old San Juan Immersive Walk: History and Culture
description:
What Makes This Tour Unique

 • Insider Stories You’ll Love – Hear captivating stories and insider insights you won’t find in guidebooks.

• Authentic Cultural Experience: Explore hidden gems, historic sites, 
 and charming streets of Old San Juan.

 pace.

title: Visit El Yunque National Forest
description: On this tour you’ll learn about out culture by hearing live documentary from a license tour guide, we will explain the history of the island from the ancestors Taino until the modern days being a US territory, will walk with you on the trail to help identify tree species and spot wildlife native to the island, weather permitting we may have and opportunity to cool off at the Rio mameyes with water temperatures average 70 degrees Fahrenheit that feels so refreshing!

images.caption: Family trip at coca falls

title: VIP San Juan Salsa Night Experience at Luxury Condado Beach
description: Experience the VIP Luxury Salsa Experience at Condado Beach, set in one of the most beautiful beachfront locations in San Juan. Unlike ordinary dance events, this experience combines salsa under the stars, the soft sand, the gentle waves, and the shimmering city lights, creating a sophisticated and unforgettable tropical atmosphere. Perfect for couples, friends, or families, it’s an exclusive, luxury experience that blends culture, fun, and stunning scenery. With limited spots for groups, this is more than just a dance—it’s a memorable night in paradise you won’t want to miss.

title: El Yunque Rainforest with Waterslides and Transport from San Juan
description:
Take time to escape from the city and dare yourself to explore El Yunque Rainforest.  Hike, river trek, rock climb, spot its flora and fauna, and enjoy its gorgeous waterfalls, natural pools, and waterslides. Along with your knowledgeable certified tour guide, you will be immersed in a wild paradise and fully infused with the vibrating energy of El Yunque's rivers and fresh clean air, just like the indigenous Taino Indians did centuries ago.

Please make sure to read all details and additional information before booking.  This tour is a moderate to strenuous activity and may not be suitable for everybody.  For a lighter version please look for our El Yunque National Half-Day tour.

images.caption: Swing from a rope or jump from a cliff into the natural pools (use of protective equipment is mandatory)

title: Discover Culebra: 2-Tank Scuba Diving
description: Nothing compares to the "weightless" exhilaration of breathing underwater. Only a diver knows the feeling! The PADI Discover Scuba Diving program lets you experience the thrill of diving under the supervision of a PADI Instructor. During your adventure, you'll master some basic concepts and scuba skills in the first dive before heading off on an open water dive in dive number 2. Your open water dive takes you as deep as 12 metres/40 feet! (Of course this depends on where you dive.) You Must know how to swim (strong and confident swimmer in the ocean), be in good health, not pregnant, and you will need 18hrs minimum to fly after scuba for safety reasons. This is just an experience to try scuba, this will not certify you.

title: Scuba Tour for Certified Divers in San Juan
description:
Scuba Dogs is a PADI 5-Star Dive Center; the only official operator with all gear, restrooms, showers, lockers and professional staff right there at the Marine Park. 

In particular, this scuba tour for certified divers is uniquely AMAZING in the sense that it's a shallow dive no deeper than 30' which allows you to explore safely without decompression issues; practice basic skills and neutral buoyancy in a protected bay; encounter a great diversity and abundance marine life including yellowtail snappers, damselfish, stingrays and turtles; and explore a diverse bottom composition including the historic fish wall, statues, roman columns, shipwreck structures, and coral reef formations.

After a colorful description of the historic and ecological value of this epic site, a professional theory review on the science of diving, and basic skills training in the shallow part of the shoreline, you'll be exploring the entire bay safely under the direct supervision of a dive professional.

title: Private Casablanca jewish heritage Tour
description:
Discover the Jewish heritage of Casablanca on a private guided tour with Typically Morocco Tours. Led by an official licensed guide with advanced academic training in Jewish heritage, this experience offers an authentic insight into Jewish life, history, and culture in Morocco.

Visit Temple Beth El, Casablanca’s most prominent synagogue, the Museum of Moroccan Judaism, the historic Mellah Jewish Quarter, the Jewish Cemetery, and Eim Habanim Synagogue, an active center of community life.

Please note: the Museum of Moroccan Judaism is closed on Saturdays and Sundays. On these days, the visit may be replaced with an exterior stop, extra heritage commentary, or another suitable Jewish heritage site, depending on timing and availability.

This private tour is ideal for families, heritage travelers, multigenerational groups, and shore excursions from Casablanca Port.

title: Transportation in San Juan, Isla verde, Condado, Hotels, Cruises & Airbnb's SJU
description:
Our Puerto Rico Shuttle Van Service is here to accommodate your transportation needs in the Old San Juan, Isla Verde, and Carolina areas only. Here's what you can expect when you land Puerto Rico:

 1. Dedicated Service: Our vehicles are strategically stationed in Old San Juan, Isla Verde, and Carolina to provide prompt and dedicated attention to travelers.

 2. Sightseeing: As you travel from the airport to your destination, you will get a glimpse of Puerto Rico's rich history and stunning views of the Atlantic Ocean.

 3. Safe and Reliable: Your safety is our top priority. Our Certified drivers follow protocols to ensure a safe and comfortable journey.

 4. Efficient cruise port transfer: If you're arriving to cruise, we offer efficient transfer to and from the cruise port, making it hassle-free for you to embark on your adventure. 
(SAN JUAN, ISLA VERDE AND CONDADO METRO AREA ONLY)
BOOKINGS OUTSIDE SAN JUAN METRO AREA WILL BE CANCELLED

images.caption: Vans

title: Multiple Taxi Services from the San Juan Airport Up to 6 People
description: Stay away from the pressure of finding a taxi when you land in San Juan and pre-book a private exchange administration to your convenience. Meet your driver at the Pilar 8 at San Juan Air Port and head directly to your Hotel Caribe Hilton In San Juan. And we make sure to take you safely.

title: San Juan Snorkeling adventure
description:
Jump in for a classic snorkeling experience from the Caribe Hilton hotel in San Juan. Our friendly guides lead you along the rock reef in search of octopus, squids, reef fish, and the elusive seahorse. No matter your age or experience, your guide provides instruction and ensures you have the time of your life as you explore the magical world beneath the surface.

When in Puerto Rico, snorkeling in San Juan is a must! The excursion departs directly from the beach without moving to any other location and has a maximum duration of one hour. This activity is not conducted from a boat. All participants walk into the ocean directly from the beach.

title: Cave, Bats and Boas Night Tour - Puerto Rico
description:
Sit in the front row to watch a unique spectacle of nature. You will visit the Mata de Plátano Nature Reserve which houses in one of its caves an estimated population of 300 thousand bats and you will have the privilege of observing the impressive exit of these from the cave, to go in search of food in the sunset. 
You will be accompanied by Certified Interpreter Guides and biologists, who will explain the different species of bats that inhabit the cave and the importance of these in nature. With the departure of the bats, you can also observe the interaction of the endangered Puerto Rican Boa, Chilabothrus inornatus, feeding on them. A unique experience of contact with nature, which has been filmed for TV documentaries. 
With your participation in this tour in the Mata de Plátano Nature Reserve, you will help its maintenance and conservation.

images.caption: The pig-snouted bat, scientifically known as Brachyphylla cavernarum, is a species native to Puerto Rico.

title: Old San Juan: Segway PT and Segway Xafari Ebike Tours
description:
Glide through Old San Juan’s 500 years of history on this 2-hour Segway PT or Segway Xafari Ebike tour, exploring the scenic route on a Segway vehicle. Known as ‘The Walled City,’ Old San Juan was founded as a military stronghold and remains the oldest city under the American flag. This small-group tour is limited to 6 riders for Segway PT and 6 riders for Segway Ebike, allowing you to zoom around effortlessly and see more than 15 attractions. Learn from an informative guide about the city’s role during the Spanish empire, World War II, and as a US territory. Capture stunning views of San Juan Bay and Castillo San Felipe Morro along the way.

*Please note* tour schedules per vehicle type and weight restrictions are listed in the 'additional information section'.

images.caption:
Segway PT tour runs at 10:30am, 

Segway Xafari tour runs at 9:00am and 1pm

title: Private Rainforest Waterfalls and Beach Hiking Tour from San Juan
description:
Enhance your stay in San Juan embarking on an extraordinary combined Beach and Forest Experience package, both including 2 of the top 5 things to in Puerto Rico.

The adventure begins at the Vanderbilt in San Juan Mar Chiquita Beach, an emerging coastal gem, where you'll engage in a unique and exclusive off-the-beaten-path hike, unveiling stunning rock formations with panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean. The journey will continue to Toro Negro Tropical Rainforest, formerly the Caribbean National Forest, offering panoramic views and a fresher and more private way of exploring compared to the more famous but both run down and overdone El Yunque National Forest. Here, discover Las Delicias waterfall, arguably one of most beautiful in Puerto Rico, and immerse yourself in a private oasis for up to 60 minutes, enjoying its cool waters and connecting with the serene beauty of nature.

title: Old San Juan Tour in Spanish
description:
Walk with me through my Old San Juan on the only tour you will find offered only in Spanish. For English you must send us a message before booking to check availability. Usually English starts at 9:30am. The 12:30pm tour is in Spanish. Make sure you bought the right one. Old San Juan is the oldest city on the island of Puerto Rico. You will learn about its history, legends, secrets and more. It is also the second oldest city in the Americas. It has been more than 500 years since its founding. The city was built and protected by walls and defended by pirates and privateers who sought to occupy it.
 We started our walking tour from Plaza Colon. We will walk through the cobblestone streets and colorful buildings. We will learn about the churches, monuments, castles, architecture, culture etc...our mission is to fall in love with our island and have a beautiful time.

title: Arecibo Indian Cave Coastal Adventure
description: An important site within indigenous Taíno culture, Cueva del Indio is home to a beautiful selection of prehistoric cave art on Puerto Rico’s Atlantic coast. Admire over 80 pre-Columbian indigenous petroglyphs made on the cave’s limestone walls between 1200 and 1500 A.D., and learn more about the island’s indigenous cultures. The scenic hike to the cave also boasts pretty coastal views and impressive grassy cliffs. You might want to join a tour that departs from San Juan—it's a great way combine your visit to the cave with other attractions in the area, such as the Cueva Ventana and the Arecibo Observatory.

We believe nature must be appreciated wholly and you cannot do that in a crowd. In El Yunque we will hike, see waterfalls, and cliff jump in blue pools of pristine, pure water. After working up a sweat and an appetite we go to our second stop: Piñones. Piñones is iconic for 3 reasons: The beaches, the food and the people. We will visit a beach that is absolutely breathtaking. Afterwards we will visit a mom-&-pop food stand where we get delicious food from the locals. This experience is like hanging out with a local who will show you the best spots.

title: BioBay Kayak Tour with San Juan Roundtrip Transportation
description:
Don’t Stress About When or Where – We Got You!

Skip the hassle of driving and let us take care of everything! With roundtrip transportation from San Juan included, all you need to do is sit back, relax, and get ready for one of Puerto Rico’s most magical adventures.

Your journey begins with a comfortable ride to the coastal town of Fajardo, where our friendly guides will greet you at Las Croabas. After a quick safety briefing, you’ll paddle through serene mangrove channels into the glowing waters of Laguna Grande.

As night falls, the magic begins — every stroke of your paddle sets the water sparkling with millions of glowing dinoflagellates, a natural wonder found in only a few places on Earth. Along the way, your guides will share fascinating stories about the bay’s delicate ecosystem, the mangroves, and even the historic Fajardo Lighthouse.

By offering transportation, we make this unique experience easy and stress-free — so you can focus on the adventure, not the logistics.

title: Half-Day: Old San Juan, Calle Cerra,Santurce, La Placita & Loíza
description:
This tour connects you with the culture, street art, history, and flavors of — all in one unforgettable day.

We begin with a drive through Old San Juan, the guide will highlight iconic landmarks like El Morro, La Fortaleza, and the colorful colonial architecture. Learn about its rich history, legends, and how the old town still pulses with local life.

Explore Santurce, San Juan’s most creative and culturally dynamic neighborhoods. Stroll down Calle Cerra, famous for its colorful murals and street art scene. Enjoy a stop at local café Dosis, visit the lively Plaza del Mercado, and drive through Miramar and Condado as you hear stories of the city’s transformation and artistic rebirth.

The final stop is Loíza, the heart of Afro-Puerto Rican culture. Head to Piñones, a vibrant coastal community famous for its music, food, and traditions. Try authentic fritters at local kiosks, and relax at Vacia Talega Beach, all while learning about the area’s deep African and Taíno heritage.

title: Private - El Yunque Rainforest Puerto Rico Tour
description:
Why settle for a big, crowded tours when you can have a Private exclusive adventure tailored for your group. Leave behind the long line waits, and jump into an unforgettable half-day express tour of El Yunque of Puerto Rico—the only tropical rainforest in the U.S.

But here's the unexpected twist: after exploring the popular spots—sliding down the exhilarating 20-foot natural water slide, swinging from a rope into a jungle pool, and cliff jumping from daring heights—your guide will lead you to a hidden part of the jungle, a secret path known only to locals.

This path takes you to a secluded lagoon surrounded by lush greenery, with cascading waterfalls that look like they belong in a postcard. You'll have time to relax, swim, and even explore a hidden cave behind the falls—a true Caribbean paradise that feels like it’s just for you.

It’s not just a tour; it’s an exclusive adventure with unexpected surprises waiting at every turn. Join us for an experience that’s anything but ordinary!

For those seeking extra adrenaline, optional cliff jumps offer the chance to leap into refreshing waters below. Prefer something more relaxed? Simply swim, enjoy the scenery, and take in the beauty of this incredible natural setting.

The adventure continues with a visit to the impressive Arenales caves, where stunning stalactites and stalagmites create a breathtaking underground landscape. Nearby, a natural waterfall provides the perfect spot to pause and capture unforgettable photos.

title: Puerto Rico Coffee Plantation & Waterfall River Tour
description:
Skip the long drives and spend more time experiencing Puerto Rico, not sitting in a van. This well-paced tour takes you into the mountains for an authentic look at the island’s coffee culture, natural beauty, and local flavors.

Visit an award-winning coffee hacienda, recognized with the People’s Choice Award at the Coffee & Chocolate Expo for three consecutive years. Enjoy a guided tour of the plantation, learn how coffee is grown and produced, and sample fresh coffee or grab breakfast from the on-site café.

Then, cool off at Río Siete Chorros, where you can swim, relax by the waterfalls, or take in the lush tropical scenery.

End the experience with an optional stop at a local restaurant to enjoy traditional Puerto Rican food and drinks before heading back.

title: Coffee & Waterfalls Tour: from the mountains to the cup
description:
Discover the magic of Puerto Rico’s central mountains on our Coffee & Waterfalls Tour in Ciales — a full-sensory adventure that combines rich coffee culture with breathtaking nature.

Nicknamed the town of coffee, Ciales is home to some of the island’s finest single-origin coffee. On this tour, you’ll visit two small, family-owned coffee farms and two small coffee shops, where you’ll learn firsthand about the coffee-making process — from bean to cup — and enjoy fresh tastings along the way.

We’ll also explore two of the area’s hidden gems: Cascada Las Delicias, a picturesque accessible waterfall tucked deep in the forest, and Charco de Doña Juana.

Whether you’re a coffee lover, nature enthusiast, or just looking to experience authentic Puerto Rico, this tour offers the perfect mix of culture, flavor, and adventure.

Small group experience 
Perfect for: Nature lovers • Coffee enthusiasts • Small groups 
Level: Easy – suitable for all fitness levels

description: If you are looking for a tour to put the icing on the cake before you leave Puerto Rico, I have the tour for you! The tombstoning or cliff jumps of this place are anywhere between 5' to 30' feet. You will jump off into a pool of fresh, refreshing, natural water, approximately 15' feet deep. If you are strong and like to hike, but do not know how to swim, LIFE JACKETS ARE PROVIDED for everyone. No one will lose out on the fun. After we are done with our Exhilarating day, we will end up at a local restaurant in the mountains. At this restaurant we will teach you what our traditional food is all about. title: Bio Bay Night Kayak Tour with Transport from San Juan
description:
Bring a little excitement to your vacation with our night kayaking tour in the Bio Bay of Laguna Grande Reserve in Fajardo. The Bio Bay has microscopic organisms called dinoflagellates that produce a glowing effect when they are stirred by our paddles. 
What should I wear?
Come prepared to get wet from your waist down. Lightweight, synthetic clothing or a swimsuit is the best. Do not walk bare foot on the beach for safety reasons.
What to bring?
Change of clothes, towel and mosquito repellent (just in case!). You can bring cameras only with water proof bag or water proof case.
What not to bring or wear?
Jeans or sweats. Don’t bring any valuables you don’t want to get wet, lose or damage.
Storage available?
Yes, Anything you bring on the water has the potential to get wet, damage or lost.
- An Eco-Adventure – For outdoor enthusiast and nature lovers only. 
- Chance to experience the glow of bioluminescent water.
- Live-commentary about the Ecosystem in English or Spanish.

title: San Juan Highlights: Private Driving Tour
description: Enjoy a personal and private introduction to the City of San Juan, the second-oldest European-founded settlement in the Americas and the oldest under US jurisdiction on this 2.5-hour sightseeing tour. Climb aboard a comfortable black vehicle and explore the highlights of the city. Ready for the Ultimate Day in Paradise? ☀️

If you're looking to experience the absolute best of Puerto Rico’s natural beauty, this full-day adventure from the San Juan area has it all! Rainforest hikes, stunning beaches, and amazing wildlife—all packed into one unforgettable tour.

Your journey kicks off in the heart of El Yunque National Forest, the only tropical rainforest in the U.S. National Park system. Get ready to immerse yourself in lush greenery, hike scenic trails, and breathe in the pure mountain air. 

Then it’s time to switch gears and head to the coast for some sun-soaked fun at Luquillo Beach. With its crystal-clear waters and soft white sands, it’s the perfect spot to swim, relax, or just soak in the Caribbean vibes. Craving something tasty? You’re in luck—nearby kiosks offer local eats, shopping, and icy-cold piña coladas.

 Two iconic destinations. One epic day.

title: Laguna Grande Night Kayaking Bio Bay and Transportation
description:
Bring a little excitement to your vacation with our night kayaking tour in the Bio Bay of Laguna Grande Reserve in Fajardo. The Bio Bay has microscopic organisms called dinoflagellates that produce a glowing effect when they are stirred by our paddles. 
What should I wear?
Come prepared to get wet from your waist down. Lightweight, synthetic clothing or a swimsuit is the best. Do not walk bare foot on the beach for safety reasons.
What to bring?
Change of clothes, towel and mosquito repellent (just in case!). You can bring cameras only with water proof bag or water proof case.
What not to bring or wear?
Jeans or sweats. Don’t bring any valuables you don’t want to get wet, lose or damage.
Storage available?
Yes, Anything you bring on the water has the potential to get wet, damage or lost.
- An Eco-Adventure – For outdoor enthusiast and nature lovers only. 
- Chance to experience the glow of bioluminescent water.
- Live-commentary about the Ecosystem in English or Spanish.

Join us on the ultimate off-the-beaten-path Tropical Rainforest and nature lover's adventure in Puerto Rico: our full-day package to Toro Negro, our State Tropical Rainforest. Recognized as one of the top 10 things to do in San Juan and a standout among Puerto Rico's top 3 experiences, this immersive journey promises to unveil the best features of the Tropical Rainforest. 

Your nature exploration begins at bustling San Juan and takes you to a scenic drive through the lush landscapes, cliffs, panoramic views and the rustic beauty of rural Puerto Rico.

At Toro Negro, discover the sheer beauty of Las Delicias waterfall, a pristine oasis where you can immerse yourself in the purest waters Puerto Rico has to offer. Continue your adventure to La Piedra Escrita in Adjuntas, a captivating rock adorned with ancient Taino Petroglyphs. After a flavorsome lunch at an authentic Puerto Rican restaurant, your journey concludes with a visit to Chorro de Doña Juana and take a dip at it's pond.

description:
Understand why Old San Juan and rum making have been an essential part of Puerto Rico’s history through centuries. At the Old San Juan sightseeing and walking tour, you will explore one of Puerto Rico’s most iconic touristic destinations, learn about its fascinating history, unique forts, impressive architecture and admire the blue cobblestones streets and charming colorful houses. 

Then enjoy a more intimate and engaging experience at the island’s premier rum distillery. Learn about the history and unique craftsmanship of Ron del Barrilito, the oldest Puerto Rican rum, still made in the same place and in the same way since 1880. You’ll see the Hacienda’s several historical landmarks, learn about the Fernández family who created Ron del Barrilito and get an in-depth walk through along the age-old process that makes Ron del Barrilito one of a kind. Tour includes a premium cocktail drink.

Minimum age is 18 years old. Minimum tour group size is 8 people.

title: The Ghosts of San Juan: Private 2.5-hour Spooky Evening Tour
description:
Your tour begins in Columbus Square, where your expert private guide will introduce you to the city’s tenuous relationship with the notorious explorer. In June 2016, the 362 foot of Columbus was unveiled 45 miles west of San Juan on a ceremonial site for the Taíno, a culture nearly driven to extinction by Columbus’s exploits. Learn why islanders are wary of Columbus’s history on their island, and why they attribute centuries of disasters to his name, which they dare not utter in completion. 

See Castillo San Cristobal (external only), which was meant to protect the city from attacks by land, and remains haunted by one of the island’s tragic love stories, reminiscent of Shakespeare’s Romeo & Juliet. Other tales include the disappearance of a Spanish soldier whose clothes and gun remained in the remote sentry box; to this day, numerous sightings of the lovers and the soldier are still being reported. 

Your private ghost tour of San Juan is 2.5 hours and is all done on foot.

title: Private African-Taino Food Tasting & Historic Old San Juan Tour
description:
First we will take you to Old San Juan where you will stroll through the city including the Capitol Building Grounds, the Walkway of the Presidents, walls and forts, and the blue cobblestone streets and celebrate our history and culture learning about it's top 90 landmarks.

Then we’ll visit Piñones, our vibrant Black Heritage area. Here, you’ll not only have the opportunity to savor African-Taino-infused street food, but also witness the art of preparing our traditional dishes in the last remaining place where items are still made right in front of you.

Using centuries-old techniques passed down by their ancestors, the true locals of this colorful community will prepare delicacies like Alcapurrias: A Taino-African fritter made from yuca and yautía; Piononos: A true African-inspired fritter, featuring a flour-coated sweet plantain nest; or Empanadillas: A Spanish-African fritter, made with a flour-based dough, all stuffed with your choice of meat or seafood and fried to perfection.

title: Half-Day: Old San Juan, Calle Cerra,Santurce, La Placita & Loíza

Explore Santurce, San Juan’s most creative and culturally dynamic neighborhoods. Stroll down Calle Cerra, famous for its colorful murals and street art scene. Enjoy a stop at local café Dosis, visit the lively Plaza del Mercado, and drive through Miramar and Condado as you hear stories of the city’s transformation and artistic rebirth.

The final stop is Loíza, the heart of Afro-Puerto Rican culture. Head to Piñones, a vibrant coastal community famous for its music, food, and traditions. Try authentic fritters at local kiosks, and relax at Vacia Talega Beach, all while learning about the area’s deep African and Taíno heritage.

description:
Bring a little excitement to your vacation with our night kayaking tour in the Bio Bay of Laguna Grande Reserve in Fajardo. The Bio Bay has microscopic organisms called dinoflagellates that produce a glowing effect when they are stirred by our paddles. 
What should I wear?
Come prepared to get wet from your waist down. Lightweight, synthetic clothing or a swimsuit is the best. Do not walk bare foot on the beach for safety reasons.
What to bring?
Change of clothes, towel and mosquito repellent (just in case!). You can bring cameras only with water proof bag or water proof case.
What not to bring or wear?
Jeans or sweats. Don’t bring any valuables you don’t want to get wet, lose or damage.
Storage available?
Yes, Anything you bring on the water has the potential to get wet, damage or lost.
- An Eco-Adventure – For outdoor enthusiast and nature lovers only.

description: In this tour, you will get more value as you experience a city tour, beach tour, cave tour, and rainforest tour all in one. Explore Puerto Rico in the comfort of a private tour. While the island itself is captivating, what makes our tour exceptional is our unmatched service. We will curate an unforgettable experience exclusively for your group, allowing you to immerse yourself in the essence of Puerto Rico by visiting the hidden gems frequented by locals, always accompanied by a licensed tour guide who will exceed your expectations. We strive to create memories that will last a lifetime.

description: A party bus is a great option for tourists and locals so they can tour the best places in the area while enjoying the music in the company of friends and family. Our buses are equipped with music, lights, video, karaoke, and our drivers will ensure that you have the best time in Puerto Rico.

title: Half-Day El Yunque National Forest Tour from San Juan
description: Sit back and relax on this leisurely half-day trip to the El Yunque National Rainforest. You'll discover an amazing world of nature, waterfalls, scenic views, and the subtropical rainforest habitat of Puerto Rico's endangered wildlife. This tour includes a 30 to 45-minute walk on a trail. We are one of the few operators that have the permits to enter the El Yunque National Rainforest. Our tour guides have special training from the Forest Service to provide the most updated information.

title: The Best of Puerto Rico Private Day Tour
description:
How much of Puerto Rico can you see on one day? 
Quite a lot. Puerto Rico is small, and has good infrastructure (road) so is easy to move from one place to the other. So, if your flight makes a long layover in PR, or if you stop on a cruise, or you want to see as much as possible in one day, this is the tour for you. 

You will get a full tour of Old San Juan (know the history and culture of Puerto Rico), a beach forest (mangroves, lagoons), a cave and a glimpse of the rain forest.

This is not a regular commercial tour. I will show you different places. I will give you a lot of interesting information in a fun/informal way. We can be spontaneous. You can get to "feel", "breath" and "enjoy" Puerto Rico.

title: Private Half-Day Tour to El Yunque Rainforest
description:
A private tour of the El Yunque Rain Forest

We will drive through the only tropical rain forest within the US. See some of the amazing views. Be amazed by the waterfalls. Do a short hike. We might even be able to swim on a river (let me know ahead of time).

This is not a regular commercial tour. I will show you different places. I will give you a lot of interesting information in a fun and informal way. You can get to "feel", "breathe," and "enjoy" the rainforest. This tour is suitable for all activity levels.

title: Laguna Grande Night Kayaking Bio Bay and Transportation
description:
Bring a little excitement to your vacation with our night kayaking tour in the Bio Bay of Laguna Grande Reserve in Fajardo. The Bio Bay has microscopic organisms called dinoflagellates that produce a glowing effect when they are stirred by our paddles. 
What should I wear?
Come prepared to get wet from your waist down. Lightweight, synthetic clothing or a swimsuit is the best. Do not walk bare foot on the beach for safety reasons.
What to bring?
Change of clothes, towel and mosquito repellent (just in case!). You can bring cameras only with water proof bag or water proof case.
What not to bring or wear?
Jeans or sweats. Don’t bring any valuables you don’t want to get wet, lose or damage.
Storage available?
Yes, Anything you bring on the water has the potential to get wet, damage or lost.
- An Eco-Adventure – For outdoor enthusiast and nature lovers only. 
- Chance to experience the glow of bioluminescent water.
- Live-commentary about the Ecosystem in English or Spanish.

title: Combo: Loiza, Cultue, Art and Bomba Dance Class on The Beach
description: This tour is the only tour that connects you directly with the people of Loíza, supporting the local economy and offering an authentic cultural exchange. We’re also the only company that includes multiple activities in a single day, so you can make the most of your visit.

title: Private Historical Walking Tour of Old San Juan
description: Every tour of this magical city is different because Old San Juan is a city filled with rich history and tall tales. We will share the history from fact as well as the tales passed down from generation to generation. Let us transport you through the power of words while guiding you around the place where it actually happened.

title: Puerto Rico Hiking and Waterfall PRIVATE Excursion!
description:
Join us on a Private Tour of the outskirts of El Yunque in a comfortable luxury vehicle (our tour does not enter El Yunke National park protected area's). On this adventure you will be able to explore off the beaten path swimming spots and waterfalls in the areas surrounding the only tropical rainforest in the United States.

* Our adventure consists of entering a local trail for approximately 25 minutes in which you will meet some of the 240 species of native plants and trees and a number of animals such as the coqui, reptiles and endemic birds.
* Immerse yourself in the refreshing natural forest pools while you photograph its spectacular waterfalls.
* Drive up to 2000 feet while enjoying the best panoramic views offered by the eastern mountain range of Puerto Rico.
* Finally, discover the authentic flavors of the island in one of our most famous fruit and juice shops in the countryside, where you can try delicious empanadas & the nutritious frapes and natural juices of the country.

title: El Yunque Rainforest, Waterslides & Beach Tour from San Juan
description:
Experience the Best of Puerto Rico on This Unforgettable Full-Day Tour!

Embark on an exciting adventure through the breathtaking El Yunque Rainforest, where you’ll explore natural waterslides, refreshing waterfalls and crystal-clear pools. After your rainforest adventure, indulge in authentic Puerto Rican cuisine at the famous Luquillo Kiosks — a lively strip of local restaurants and bars. Finally, unwind and soak up the sun at the stunning Luquillo Beach.

Why Book With Us?

 (maximum 14 guests)

title: Small Group Early Bird El Yunque Rainforest Tour
description:
Small Groups:
Why settle for big, crowded tours, when you can have an exclusive small group adventure tailored just for you. Leave behind the typical long waits, and jump into an unforgettable half-day express tour of El Yunque, the only Puerto Rico Caribbean tropical rainforest in the U.S.
But here's the unexpected twist: after exploring the popular spots—sliding down the exhilarating 20-foot natural water slide, swinging from a rope into a jungle pool, and cliff jumping from daring heights—your guide will lead you to a hidden part of the jungle, a secret path known only to locals.

This path takes you to a El Yunque Rainforest secluded lagoon with cascading waterfalls that look like they belong in a postcard. You'll have time to relax, swim, and even explore a hidden cave behind the falls—a true jungle paradise that feels like it’s just for you. 
 It’s an exclusive adventure with unexpected surprises waiting at every turn. Join us for an experience that’s anything but ordinary!

title: Puerto Rico Waterfall Hiking Tour: Discover Hidden Gems 2.0
description:
TRUST ME WHEN I SAY, that we created this true hidden waterfall tour. On this tour of Puerto Rico’s Hidden Waterfall 2.0, you get to go Bouldering, dirt climbing on true mountain trails within the mountains of the island of Puerto Rico.

The highlight of the day is a 150 foot waterfall for you to get under and receive a natural massage from. The trail is approximately 30 minutes long, depending on your fitness level. THIS IS NOT A BEGINNER’S HIKE, but on the same note, its not super impossible either. This tour has everything that you look forward to on a real true hike in the woods. 

 After having an unforgettable time at the waterfall and once returning to the van, we will take you to an authentic Puerto Rican restaurant to enjoy lunch (at your own expense). Learn from your guide about the food we eat in the countryside of Puerto Rico. Upon finishing your meal and settling up your bill, we will drive you safely back to your place of stay.

title: Private MEGA El Yunque Full Day Tour: Rainforest, Rivers, Beaches
description:
Let's explore Puerto Rico's most amazing northeastern region and its 5 unique municipalities on our full-day private or semi-private (up to 6 pax. max.)cultural tour with a certified guide Professor Nat!
The landscapes and ecosystems are mind-boggling.The Northeast covers coastal forests, seagrass beds, coral reefs, and pristine beaches.
We'll start with exotic beach food emporia in Piñones, tasting the famous authentic 'fritura'. Next, mysterious Loiza will transport us across the ocean to a world of African culture. Enjoy a local barbecue lunch to go (extra charge, picnic style). 
Then, to El Yunque forest private terrains in Rio Grande, where we'll hike through the lush tropical forest to the sacred river bath for the Taino Indians.
A yummy natural local fruits frappe and juice bar is next, to keep that healthy vibe going!
Finally, we'll head to Luquillo, Puerto Rico's finest beach. You can indulge in the finest sand and turquoise waters.
The itinerary order is subject to change.

images.caption: I'll bring special educational activities for kids!

title: El Yunque National Forest: The Road 191 Corridor - Half Day
description:
This tour includes the El Yunque National Forest Park Pass into the 191 corridor, past the Forest Service’s Check Point in Rio Grande, Puerto Rico.

See the 85 foot La Coca Falls, climb the 70 foot tall Yokahu Tower – at 1, 500 feet in elevation, walk at a natural trail with its mountain palms and tree ferns. Finish with the opportunity of submerging yourself with a river swim.

images.caption: La Coca Fall

title: Private Puerto Rican Cooking Class in San Juan for Groups
description: Enjoy a private cooking class in a local San Juan home in a residential neighborhood away from the touristy center. This is a perfect experience for anyone looking to connect with local Puerto Rican culture over food. Your cooking class takes place in a local home kitchen where Yeira will teach you 4-5 traditional Puerto Rican dishes. After cooking, enjoy your meal together with this warm and friendly local, passionate about food and travel. Experience authentic Puerto Rican life and cuisine with your host Yeira as you cook together and dine on authentic family recipes you won't find in restaurants. Yeira is a fun local host with a doctorate in clinical psychology who believes that food and cooking promotes wellness in people! She is excited to share her authentic Puerto Rican cuisine and culture with those visiting her hometown of San Juan.

images.caption: Puerto Rican cooking class with a local expert

title: Explore Cueva del Indio + La Poza del Obispo Beach
description:
Discover The Puerto Rico’s most breathtaking coastal adventure! This unforgettable tour begins at Cueva del Indio, where you’ll walk across the dramatic 7 natural rock arches overlooking the Atlantic Ocean. Then, descend into the sacred cave to see ancient Taíno petroglyphs carved into the stone, connecting to the island’s first inhabitants.

Then to La Poza del Obispo. A breathtaking beach known for its natural ocean pool surrounded by dramatic rock formations. These rocks help break the force of the Atlantic waves, creating a shallow area that’s ideal for a refreshing swim. This beach is a favorite among locals and visitors for its golden sand, turquoise waters, and scenic views of the nearby Arecibo Lighthouse. 

To finish, enjoy a flavorful stop at local food trucks offering authentic Puerto Rican cuisine—perfect for tasting the island like a local.

This tour is the perfect mix of culture, adventure, beach time, and food—all in one unforgettable experience!

title: Private El Yunque Tour To Hidden Waterfall Remote Area
description: El Yunque was believed to be the living quarters of Yocahu, the most powerful Taino god, and it is easy to see why! El Yunque is exceptionally beautiful, pristine, and untouched by humans. Come explore with me this remote and hidden part of El Yunque, away from the crowds of "The Slides". Visit El Yunque like a true local and come get lost with me in this unbelievably beautiful oasis of life. Here, we will visit the Holy Spirit River, where we will hike to three pools and countless waterfalls. We will be able to sit and receive a back massage with hydrotherapy as the waterfall beats down on our backs. Come experience the real Puerto Rico and the El Yunque National Forest away from the crowds and the tourist traps.

title: Indian Cave & La Planta Waterfalls
description:
What sets this adventure apart is the expertise of our certified local interpreter, who brings deep knowledge of the area and its rich history. Join us for an off-the-beaten-path hiking and caving experience.

Your safety is our top priority. We are committed to creating a secure, comfortable, and enjoyable environment while guiding you through Puerto Rico’s rich history, vibrant culture, and breathtaking landscapes.

Puerto Rico is an incredible destination, and experiencing it with knowledgeable locals makes it even more special. Join us to uncover the real stories, traditions, and hidden gems of this beautiful island while supporting small local businesses.

title: Sacred Rainforest and Archaeological Experience
description: Explore the rainforest of the central mountain region and experience the Neolithic world of the Taínos in this private, full day tour! After your guide picks you up from your hotel at 8am, you will be driven west, about 30 minutes, to mountain town of Ciales. Once there, you will explore the central mountain region of the Toro Negro Rainforest, the Tres Picachos National Forest, visit the pure waters of Las Delicias Waterfall and lastly, the sacred archaeological site of the Zama petroglyph mural . This is a full day experience approximately 8 hours in length with lunch included, with a maximum group of 6, and the tour concludes with a drive back to your hotel.

title: Puerto Rican Salsa Class and Night Out in San Juan, Puerto Rico
description: Best dance class around. Learn everything from Puerto Rican Salsa and our culture. Learn basic steps and a little bit of history. After the class, we are going to practice the salsa steps on a local spot.

title: Private Historic Old San Juan Sightseeing and Walking Experience
description:
If there is a tour every visitor and traveler must do before even trying to walk our old city’s street, this will be it!

This tour was curated with comfort and as little walking as posible in mind, and it is the single tour every and anyone should do to avoid wasting hours looking for the landmarks, buildings and walking under the scorching sun trying to discover it’s history. 

In it your driver and guide will take you in a route through the 80+ main buildings and landmarks and a journey in time telling you about the city’s history in short and precise way as he drives you slowly through the narrow and colorful streets of our historic walled city.

Depending the lenght you choose, you will have from 2 to 6 stops to take pictures and get a closer look of the main landmarks of the city. Among them San Cristobal and El Morro Forts, El Convento, Ballaja, Saint John’s Cathedral, La Rogativa, Del Cristo and San Jose chapels and Colombus and Ponce de Leon square.

title: El Yunque Rainforest Full Day Private Tour
description:
Because this is a private tour, everything is up to you. You can chose where you want to go, how long you want to stay up there, etc.
If you are avid walkers, we can go to the top of El Yunque Peak, Mount Britton, Tres Picachos, The Rock, etc. From up there we can see both, the Atlantic Ocean and the Caribbean Sea. We will walk through the cloud forest. 
You don't have to wait for other people. You don't have to rush either. You can stop to take pictures if you like (but don't get your camera wet!). If you get tired we can stop. If you want to go faster, we can do that too.

This is not a regular commercial tour. I will show you different places. I will give you a lot of interesting information in a fun/informal way. You can get to "feel", "breath" and "enjoy" the rainforest.

"Private Tours of Old San Juan" operates under Special Use Permit from the USDA Forest Service on the El Yunque National Forest, permit number YNF-22061

title: Old San Juan & Loiza African Town Full-Day Tour: Minimum 8+people
description:
Immerse yourself in our fascinating history and rich culture with this experience. Visit the Historical Site of Old San Juan, and one of oldest towns in America, for a sightseeing and walking tour. Learn about its history, unique forts, impressive architecture and admire its blue cobblestones streets and charming colorful houses. 

Then discover the influence of our African heritage at the picturesque little town of Loíza, Capital of our African Tradition. Start with a beautiful coastal drive and authentic lunch at the beachfront kiosks of Piñones. Later meet a renowned painter and artist at his house and workshop and stop at an African Mask Store. End your day having fun and learning about our Bomba Afro-Caribbean music joining a class by recognized local musicians and dance to the beat of drums. 

Minimum age is 2 years old. Minimum tour group size is 8 people.

title: Private Rainforest Tour with Transportation
description:
Connect with nature and get a dose of fresh air in this fun tour to Las Tinajas in Ceiba, Puerto Rico. The clear trails make this a very family-friendly adventure!

We will hike an off the beaten path trail for 20-30. At this path there are plenty of exotic plants and birds to see, the guide will share all the details about the flora and fauna of this amazing place. At the Tinajas natural pool and waterfalls, you will be able to swim on crystal clear waters and have an option to adventure on a swinging rope, or even jump up to 30 feet high rocks. We will then proceed to another 5-10 hike to a natural pool with a 20 feet natural WATERSLIDE and with an optional 3 feet high jump from a rock.

After the adventure at El Yunque, we will take you to a mom and pop restaurant with food made with Puerto Rican love!

title: San Juan Old Walking Tour
description: Puerto Rico was part of the Spanish colony until 1898, where it became part of the United States. We are a country rich in history and culture. You will learn the history of the archipelago, immersed between the walls of the old city, which received colonial attacks, struggles and wars. We'll be walking around and listening to the story in real time. The idea is that you will know the Isle of Charm from its first inhabitants until our update. It'll be fun and interesting. This immersive tour of the walls of the old city will make you feel that you are still alive in the time of the conquest. You will have the opportunity to take pictures with amazing views. In addition to the beaches, we have green landscapes, history and many corners of Old San Juan to know. Feel free to book with Oasis Tour Line!

title: El Yunque National Forest and Luquillo Kiosks Combo
description:
Gain exclusive access to El Yunque Rainforest Park, thanks to our outfitter status and special use permits. No capacity closures will interrupt your experience.
Expert Guidance: Let our passionate guides lead the way as they share insights into the biodiversity, cultural significance of El Yunque. Get ready for a journey filled with fascinating discoveries!
Efficient Pickup Locations: Say goodbye to wasted time with our strategically located pickup spots. We've planned everything to ensure minimal stops, so you can maximize your adventure time.
Natural Pool Experience: Take a refreshing dip in the pristine waters of a natural pool formed by the Mameyes River (weather permitting). It's the perfect way to cool off and connect with nature.
Combo Tour: Extend your adventure beyond the rainforest with a visit to the iconic Luquillo Kiosks Beach. Indulge in local cuisine and soak up the sun on the beautiful beachfront. It's the ultimate way to unwind after a day of exploration.

title: Hidden Gems of the North, Puerto Rico Private Tour
description: Come and discover with us the hidden gems of the north coast of Puerto Rico. Starting with a simple hike near the beach to discover a stunning little beach where you will be able to see an open cave called "Cueva de las Golondrinas" or Swallow Cave, known like this for the abundance of these birds near the cave. After that we will venture to enjoy a short easy hike to find a small crystal clear water spring. Once we enjoy a nice swim in the spring we will head to the last attraction, a natural cave full of life and amazing things to see. At the end of the service an optional stop to get something to eat will be offered.

title: Western Island Full Day Tour
description:
Sparkling waters passing through polished rocks of two separate waterfalls, deep inside a lush green forest with warm rays of sunshine peeking through, this is truly special place to connect with the magic and beauty of nature. Be sure to bring your swimming gear and water shoes, this stop you are almost certain to get wet.

Other Sights/Stops:

Nestled in the limestone-rich area of Vega Baja are clear, fresh-water springs and pools; common gathering spots for locals to cool off on warmer days. Larger pools, such as Manantial Ojo de Agua, are brightly decorated and colorful, providing unique photo opportunities for you and your group.

There are many amazing beach stops on this tour, ideal for lounging in the sun, soaking or snorkeling in the typically calm waters (spotting colorful tropical fish, coral and other small sea life). The view from Arecibo lighthouse, near La Playa Poza del Obispo, is equally amazing - especially if you're lucky enough to catch it near sunset!

description:
Experience our Private Excursion tailored exclusively for you!

Embark on a serene journey through the picturesque landscapes as you make your way to the heart of the city. Upon your arrival, meet your seasoned tour guide, ready to lead you on an enchanting tour of island of Culebra.

Discover the awe-inspiring allure of island of Culebra, marvel at the grandeur of Luis Peña, and capture iconic moments.

title: Full Day Eastern Island Tour
description:
Sand-Covered Beaches
This area has so many amazing beaches to explore, including but not limited to: Faro de las Cabezas de San Juan (which includes a lighthouse, dry forest, Playa Lirios), Playa Colora, Playa Escondida, Balneario Seven Seas, and the breath-taking Balneario la Monserrate (Luquillo Beach).

Rich & Colorful Culture
Known for their strong Afro-Puerto Rican art, music, dance, and culinary traditions, the colorful town of Loíza is a must-see for those visiting the area. Other points of interest open year-round include the gorgeous Playa la Posita, Pinones State Forest and Punta Cangrejo.

El Yunque Rainforest
With an average rainfall of 120 inches a year, El Yunque has a very diverse ecosystem, hosting hundreds of unique plant and animal species, including the Island's famous coquí frog. From hiking various paths and trails, to riding down natural (rock) waterslides, or finding a hidden tide pool to sit and soak it all in, this stop alone is worth the trip!

title: Hidden Gems of the Central Mountains: Tropical Forest Waterfalls
description: Don’t go chasing waterfalls? why not?! This tour will take you to some of the most breathtaking views and waterfalls the Cordillera Central has to offer. The Cordillera Central is the spinal cord of Puerto Rico, located approximately 3,000 feet above sea level. Experience the culture of the mountainside of the Island, savor the rich gastronomy, and take a dip at the deliciously refreshing waterfalls like Toro Negro forest, Toro Negro River, Las Delicias, and more!

title: Utuado Canyon, River & Waterfall Adventure in Puerto Rico
description:
Enjoy a unique full-day experience and immerse yourself into the lushness of the central mountain region of the island. Prepare for a scenic drive through the countryside roads, spectacular mountains, and panoramic views to find one of the most unique and mystical places on the island. 

River trek to a serene and secluded water pool and be astounded by a hidden canyon leading to a beautiful waterfall. Marvel at the stunning towering volcanic rock cliffs uniquely sculpted by the passing of the river and admire a majestic mountain at a distance completing this picture-perfect setting. Finally, explore the area and find petroglyphs carved into rocks centuries ago by our Taino ancestors and sense the connection they had with nature.

This is a challenging tour that requires a certain degree of physical fitness, balance, river trekking skills and basic swimming skills. Closed sturdy water hiking shoes are required. 

MINIMUM AGE is 10 years old. Minimum tour group size is 4 people.

title: El Yunque Rainforest Morning River and Hike Adventure
description:
Experience the wonders of nature and breathe in the fresh air on a 7-hour tour to Las Tinajas in Ceiba, Puerto Rico. The well-defined trails make this adventure ideal for families. 

Embark on an off-the-beaten-path hike lasting 20-30 minutes, where exotic plants and birds abound. Your guide will provide fascinating insights into the flora and fauna of this remarkable location. At the Tinajas natural pool and waterfalls, plunge into crystal-clear waters and enjoy swinging from a rope or leaping from rocks up to 15 feet high. Join us on the ultimate off-the-beaten-path Tropical Rainforest and nature lover's adventure in Puerto Rico: our full-day package to Toro Negro, our State Tropical Rainforest. Recognized as one of the top 10 things to do in San Juan and a standout among Puerto Rico's top 3 experiences, this immersive journey promises to unveil the best features of the Tropical Rainforest. 

Your nature exploration begins at bustling San Juan and takes you to a scenic drive through the lush landscapes, cliffs, panoramic views and the rustic beauty of rural Puerto Rico.

At Toro Negro, discover the sheer beauty of Las Delicias waterfall, a pristine oasis where you can immerse yourself in the purest waters Puerto Rico has to offer. Continue your adventure to La Piedra Escrita in Adjuntas, a captivating rock adorned with ancient Taino Petroglyphs. description:
Understand why Old San Juan and rum making have been an essential part of Puerto Rico’s history through centuries. At the Old San Juan sightseeing and walking tour, you will explore one of Puerto Rico’s most iconic touristic destinations, learn about its fascinating history, unique forts, impressive architecture and admire the blue cobblestones streets and charming colorful houses. 

Then enjoy a more intimate and engaging experience at the island’s premier rum distillery. Learn about the history and unique craftsmanship of Ron del Barrilito, the oldest Puerto Rican rum, still made in the same place and in the same way since 1880. You’ll see the Hacienda’s several historical landmarks, learn about the Fernández family who created Ron del Barrilito and get an in-depth walk through along the age-old process that makes Ron del Barrilito one of a kind. Tour includes a premium cocktail drink.

Minimum age is 18 years old. Minimum tour group size is 8 people.

title: The Ghosts of San Juan: Private 2.5-hour Spooky Evening Tour
description:
Your tour begins in Columbus Square, where your expert private guide will introduce you to the city’s tenuous relationship with the notorious explorer. In June 2016, the 362 foot of Columbus was unveiled 45 miles west of San Juan on a ceremonial site for the Taíno, a culture nearly driven to extinction by Columbus’s exploits. Learn why islanders are wary of Columbus’s history on their island, and why they attribute centuries of disasters to his name, which they dare not utter in completion. 

See Castillo San Cristobal (external only), which was meant to protect the city from attacks by land, and remains haunted by one of the island’s tragic love stories, reminiscent of Shakespeare’s Romeo & Juliet. Other tales include the disappearance of a Spanish soldier whose clothes and gun remained in the remote sentry box; to this day, numerous sightings of the lovers and the soldier are still being reported. 

Your private ghost tour of San Juan is 2.5 hours and is all done on foot.

description:
First we will take you to Old San Juan where you will stroll through the city including the Capitol Building Grounds, the Walkway of the Presidents, walls and forts, and the blue cobblestone streets and celebrate our history and culture learning about it's top 90 landmarks.

Then we’ll visit Piñones, our vibrant Black Heritage area. Here, you’ll not only have the opportunity to savor African-Taino-infused street food, but also witness the art of preparing our traditional dishes in the last remaining place where items are still made right in front of you.

Using centuries-old techniques passed down by their ancestors, the true locals of this colorful community will prepare delicacies like Alcapurrias: A Taino-African fritter made from yuca and yautía; Piononos: A true African-inspired fritter, featuring a flour-coated sweet plantain nest; or Empanadillas: A Spanish-African fritter, made with a flour-based dough, all stuffed with your choice of meat or seafood and fried to perfection.

title: EL Yunque Private Tour Waterfalls and Waterslide Beach
description:
Take a ride outside of the tourist district along the east coast of Puerto Rico. As the cityscape fades, the scenery transforms into rolling hills, small towns, and dense tropical vegetation.

Upon arrival, embark on a guided hike through vibrant rainforest trails, surrounded by towering trees, exotic plants, and the sounds of coquí frogs and birds echoing through the canopy.

As the trail unfolds, you'll discover stunning cascading waterfalls. Embark on a hike to two natural rock water slides and experience the thrill of sliding into crystal-clear river pools. Enjoy the excitement of multiple cliff jumps ranging in height, and feel the rush as you swing through the air from the iconic Tarzan rope swing.

Once we finish the trail we will pack up and head to Luquillo beach where travers can shop from the 60 kiosk available in the area and swim at the beach.(4k drone footage available only raw footage no edits)
